findings.evidence: Mathlib has `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv : A[M × N] ≃ₐ[R] A[N][M]` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/Basic.lean:165`, `MonoidAlgebra.commAlgEquiv` at line 351, and `MonoidAlgebra.scalarTensorEquiv : A ⊗[R] R[M] ≃ₐ[A] A[M]` at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MonoidAlgebra.lean:91`; the PR’s line 111 builds the same algebra equivalence manually with `AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom`.
findings.fix: Define the algebra equivalence by composing `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv`, `MonoidAlgebra.commAlgEquiv` if needed, and `(MonoidAlgebra.scalarTensorEquiv R (MonoidAlgebra R G)).symm`/orientation adjustments, then prove the `single` simp lemmas from that composition before promoting to `BialgEquiv`.
findings.issue: `prodTensorAlgEquiv` reconstructs the product-monoid-algebra/tensor-product algebra equivalence from scratch instead of reusing Mathlib’s existing nested monoid-algebra and scalar-extension equivalences.

summary: The bialgebra-level statement is new, but the algebra equivalence is largely a reimplementation of existing Mathlib monoid-algebra/tensor-product equivalences. Rebuild the algebra equivalence from those APIs and keep only the bialgebra compatibility layer here.
